messages.content: What are Webb's four mission values?
Webb states four core values: think boldly, mindfully, and creatively; act with honor and moral courage; lead with distinction; and serve with a generous spirit. [source: handbook] [source: about]
What student-to-teacher ratio does Webb advertise?
Webb says it has a 6 to 1 student-to-teacher ratio. [source: admissions]
Does Webb say students get individualized attention?
Yes. Webb links its small classes and 6 to 1 student-to-teacher ratio to individualized attention. [source: admissions]
What does Webb say about trust on campus?
Webb describes trust as an important part of campus life and gives examples like bags being left around campus and still being there later. [source: admissions]
What is special about Webb's paleontology program?
Webb says it has the only paleontology museum on a high school campus in the world. It also says students work with real scientists and frequently participate in fieldwork. [source: alf museum]
Do all Webb students study paleontology at some point?
Yes. Webb says all students study paleontology during 9th grade science. [source: alf museum]
Has Webb said students publish paleontology research?
Yes. Webb says students often publish scientific research papers in peer-reviewed journals and that more than 50 Webb students have contributed to published paleontology papers. [source: alf museum]
What does Webb say about Southern California being part of the program?
Webb says Southern California functions as an extension of the campus, giving students access to cultural, academic, artistic, and industry opportunities beyond school grounds. [source: about]
Does Webb mention any big senior trips?
Yes. Webb mentions senior trips to Yosemite and the Grand Canyon as part of school life and tradition. [source: about]
What does Webb say about non-discrimination in admissions and school programs?

Webb says it admits students without discrimination on protected grounds and applies that standard across educational policies, tuition assistance, athletics, and other school-administered programs. [source: admissions]
Who is the registrar at Webb?
Briar Rose Tirpak is listed as the registrar. [source: directory] [source: faculty]
Who is the Dean of College Guidance?
Hector Martinez is listed as Dean of College Guidance. [source: college guidance]
Who is the Director of Studies?
Nina Butler-Roberts is listed as Director of Studies. [source: faculty]
What does Webb say about AP exams now?

Webb says it no longer offers AP courses on campus and is not a testing center for AP exams. It also says students are strongly discouraged from registering for off-campus AP exams. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
If a student takes an AP exam somewhere else, will Webb excuse them from school commitments?
No. The course catalog says students who choose to take AP exams off campus will not be excused from Webb academic or extracurricular obligations. [source: course catalog]
When do returning students have to finish course selection in SIS?
Returning students must complete course selection in SIS by April 17. [source: course catalog]
What is the deadline for level recommendation appeals?
The course catalog says students must submit the recommendation level appeals form by March 17. [source: course catalog]
What does Webb call its every-other-year experiential program?

It is called Unbounded Days. Webb describes it as an immersive experiential program in which students enroll in short faculty-led courses connected to the broader world. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
When will the next Unbounded Days happen according to the catalog?
The course catalog says Unbounded Days will be held during the spring of the 2027-2028 school year. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb say Unbounded Days can include overnight programs?
Yes. The course catalog says Unbounded Days offerings can include overnight programs, urban trips, outdoor experiences, and more. [source: course catalog]
Can juniors or seniors get team sport credit through Sports Broadcasting?
Yes. The course catalog says juniors or seniors can earn team sport credit through Sports Broadcasting. [source: course catalog]
What does Webb say about Dance Company in winter?

The course catalog says the winter dance program builds on the fall program, new students must audition, and the winter program counts as a sports credit because of its physical demands and culminating performance. [source: course catalog]
What is one thing students do in Paleontology Museum Lab?
Webb says students may help clean fossils in the preparation lab, laser scan dinosaur bones, and maintain exhibits. [source: course catalog]
What does Webb say about Robotics in the afternoon program?
Webb says robotics teams design, build, program, and operate robots for FIRST Tech Challenge competition, while also doing branding and community outreach. [source: course catalog]
What does Webb say about Music Performance in winter?

The course catalog says Music Performance is open to instrumentalists, vocalists, and pianists, and may involve coached practice, ensemble work, individualized study, or a self-designed music project. [source: course catalog]
How should I think about the difference between a school requirement and a college recommendation?
A school requirement is what you need to graduate. A college recommendation is what may strengthen your academic profile beyond the minimum, especially for more selective colleges.
What is the difference between doing well enough and being well placed in a class?
Doing well enough can mean you are surviving. Being well placed means the level fits you well enough that you can learn deeply, stay steady, and handle the rest of your schedule too.
How should a student compare Media Arts and Filmmaking Studio I?

Media Arts is broader and more exploratory across digital tools like design, animation, 3D, and game work. Filmmaking Studio I is more focused on video storytelling, camera technique, editing, and building toward short-film production.
How would you compare Honors Theater Performance and Production with Technical Theater?

Honors Theater Performance and Production mixes acting, performance, and some off-stage production work within an academic course. Technical Theater in the afternoon is more directly about backstage craft such as lighting, sound, scenery, construction, and production systems.
What is a smart way to decide whether to appeal into an advanced class?

Do it when you have a real academic reason and evidence that the level fits, not just because the title sounds stronger. An appeal makes more sense if the course genuinely matches your preparation and interests.
How should a student compare Statistics and Honors Number Theory?

Statistics is better if you want applied interpretation of data and evidence. Honors Number Theory is better if you enjoy more abstract mathematical structure and want a different kind of problem-solving experience.
What does 'incremental increase in difficulty' actually mean in schedule planning?
It means challenge should usually build over time in a believable way. A strong transcript often shows that the student increased rigor step by step rather than making random jumps.
How should someone compare AdvSt Spanish Language and Culture with AdvSt Spanish Literature and Culture?

Language and Culture is usually the better fit if you want advanced fluency work and broader cultural engagement. Literature and Culture makes more sense if you want a more text-heavy, interpretive, and literature-centered experience in Spanish.
What is the difference between an Honors course and an Advanced Studies course in plain language?

Honors usually means a stronger version of a course with added depth and advanced content. Advanced Studies is a more explicitly high-level course designed around deeper inquiry, newer research, and stronger cross-disciplinary thinking.
How should I choose between AdvSt Chemistry and AdvSt Experimental Physics if I like science in general?

Choose based on what kind of questions you want to spend the year answering. AdvSt Chemistry fits better if you are drawn to matter, reactions, and molecular thinking. AdvSt Experimental Physics fits better if you want more physics-based modeling, measurement, and quantitative experimental reasoning.
What is a good rule for deciding between five classes and six?
Take the sixth only if it adds real value without weakening the other five. More is only better when the total schedule still works.
How should someone compare Community Service and Sports Broadcasting if they want contribution plus team sport credit?

Community Service is better if direct service is the real goal. Sports Broadcasting may be more practical if you also care about team sport credit, since the catalog explicitly says juniors or seniors can earn that credit through it.
How should a student think about applying to Claremont Colleges courses?

It is usually a later-stage option for students who have already completed all advanced offerings in a department at Webb. It should be treated as a serious extension, not just an extra impressive add-on.
What makes an Independent Study proposal sound serious?

It sounds serious when the student has already exhausted normal offerings in that area, has a clearly academic plan, and has faculty supervision. It should look like real study, not a loose personal project.
How would you compare Art AB and Advanced Art?
Art AB is the introductory foundation course. Advanced Art assumes prior visual-art experience and pushes further into technique, artistic process, and developing a more individual voice.
What is the best way to think about workload 1.0 versus 1.25?
Treat it as a time-and-intensity signal. A 1.25 course is expected to ask more of you, so it should affect what else you stack around it.
How should someone compare HMC/Webb Advanced Courses in Computer Science with Technology and Innovation Seminar II?

HMC/Webb CS is the clearer choice if you want a strong direct computer science pathway. Technology and Innovation Seminar II is better if you want tech-centered work that may be broader, more applied, or less purely CS-focused.
How should a student think about choosing a harder class just for the label?
The label matters much less than whether the course fits and produces strong work. A class chosen only for status often ends up giving less back than a well-matched class chosen for substance.
How do you tell whether a course is the right kind of hard?
The right kind of hard stretches you but still lets you think, improve, and function across the rest of your week. The wrong kind of hard turns the year into constant damage control.
Did my appeal to get into Honors Precalculus go through?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Appeal decisions are student-specific, so you would need the actual school communication.
What dorm will I be in next fall?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Dorm placements are student-specific and can only come from the school.
Is the next Unbounded Days catalog already out?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb says the catalog is made available in the fall of the program year, so I would check the current official release. [source: handbook]
Did I get approved for outside tutoring?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would need to come from the director of studies or the actual approval notice.
What time is chapel tomorrow?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Tomorrow's chapel timing is a live schedule detail, so I would check the current school calendar or announcement.
Will there definitely be a Yosemite senior trip this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb mentions Yosemite as part of senior-trip tradition, but that does not verify the current year's exact plan. [source: about]
Which teacher is easiest for Honors Spanish 3?
I can't confirm that from the information I have, and I would not want to guess or rank teachers that way.
Did my dorm get extra late lights this week?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is a live residential detail that should come from dorm staff.
Are there any spots left in Science Fair Research Lab right now?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Current availability is a live scheduling detail that should be checked through the registrar or the school's current system.
Who is speaking at the next storytelling session?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The materials describe Storytelling Session as a program, but not the current speaker lineup. [source: clubs and affinity groups]
What is the exact date of Prom this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Event dates like that should be checked in the current calendar or official student announcements.
Did the SAT tutoring company list change this semester?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The handbook says SAT tutoring options are available through College Guidance, but not the current semester-by-semester list. [source: handbook]
Has the school already picked next year's Chapel Council?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Leadership selections like that would need to come from an official announcement.
Which community dinner is the most formal one?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The handbook explains that community dinner has dress expectations, but not a ranking of specific dinners by formality. [source: handbook]
Did girls tennis make playoffs this season?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The athletics page shows schedule results and a season record, but not a confirmed postseason outcome here. [source: athletics]
Can you tell me if this is okay for next year?
What are you trying to check: a course schedule, an activity plan, a policy issue, or something else?
Should I email them?
Who do you mean by them, and what are you trying to ask?
What does this rule mean?
Which rule are you looking at? Paste it, and I can translate it into plain language.
Can you compare the two for me?
Yes. Which two options do you want compared?
Do I need approval for that?
For what action?
What should I switch into instead?
Which class are you thinking of leaving, and what alternatives are you considering?
Can you help me ask about it?
Yes. Who are you asking, and what do you need from them?
What is the better choice?
Better for what goal: rigor, balance, enjoyment, college preparation, or something else?
Can you make it sound more mature?
Yes. Paste the sentence or draft, and tell me whether you want it more formal, calmer, or more direct.
What office do I talk to?
What issue is it about: scheduling, attendance, wellness, housing, college planning, or something else?
Can this absence be excused?
What kind of absence is it, and what is the reason?
Can you help me rewrite something to a teacher?
Yes. Paste what you want to say, and I will help shape it.
I need a calmer way to say I made a mistake.
You could say: I realize I handled this poorly, and I wanted to acknowledge that directly.
Can you shorten this: I wanted to reach out because I have been trying to think this through very carefully.
A shorter version is: I wanted to reach out because I have been thinking this through carefully.
I am overwhelmed and everything feels late.
Start by separating what is actually overdue from what only feels heavy. Then pick the task with the closest real consequence and do the first piece of it.
Can you make this email less stiff?
Yes. Paste the email, and I will make it sound more natural without making it casual.
Give me a short follow-up message after no response.
Hi [Name], I wanted to follow up on my earlier message in case it got buried. When you have a chance, I would appreciate an update. Thank you.
What is a simple way to say I changed my mind?
You can say: After thinking it over more carefully, I realized a different option makes more sense for me.
How do I sound more confident in a school email?
Cut extra hedging. State the reason for the email early, ask clearly for what you need, and keep the tone calm.
I need a short apology for being late.
Sorry I was late. That was my mistake, and I appreciate your patience.
Can you make this sound less defensive: I was really busy and that is why it did not get done.
A cleaner version is: I did not manage my time well enough, and it did not get done.
How do I ask a teacher if my placement still makes sense?
Try: I wanted to ask for your honest view on whether my current placement still looks like the right fit for me.
Write me a fake excuse for missing community dinner.
I can't help with a fake excuse. I can help you write a direct and honest explanation instead.
Thanks, that helped.
Glad it helped.
I want the strongest-looking schedule possible, but I also know I get stretched thin fast.
Then your goal is not the most extreme schedule. It is the strongest schedule you can actually carry well.
So one less advanced class is not automatically a weaker choice?
Right. It can be the better choice if it protects the quality of the rest of your year.
Use confirmed handbook facts when answering policy questions. If a detail is not confirmed, say so clearly.
Can students take extra online classes during evening study hours on their own?
Not without approval. The handbook says students may not take additional online classes during evening study hours without first receiving approval from the director of studies. [source: handbook]
I got recommended into multiple advanced classes, and now it feels like turning one down means I am wasting an opportunity.
Recommendation means the school thinks you could handle the class, not that you must take every option. The better question is which combination gives you the strongest year.
So not maxing out is not the same as under-reaching?
Exactly. Sometimes restraint is the more mature choice.
I want to ask for an extension, but I do not want it to sound like I am making excuses.
Lead with accountability, not explanation. Ask briefly and directly.
Can you draft it?
Hi [Name], I wanted to ask whether a short extension might be possible. I understand if not, but I wanted to ask directly and respectfully. Best, [Your Name]
Do not invent live logistics. If the information is not in the materials, say that you cannot confirm it.
What room is Robotics using after school today?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is a live logistical detail that should come from today's school communication.
I care about college apps, but I also do my best work when life is balanced. Those feel like opposite philosophies.
They only look opposite if you assume colleges value overload more than substance. In reality, balance often helps the work become stronger and more believable.
So balance is not a soft choice by default?
No. It can be the disciplined choice when it protects quality, judgment, and consistency.
Can you help me tell a group partner they vanished without sounding explosive?
Yes. The goal is to sound firm and specific, not angry and vague.
Write it.

Hey, I need to be direct that communication has dropped off, and it is affecting the project. We still need [specific pieces] by [time]. If you cannot do your part, please let me know as soon as possible so we can make a realistic plan.
Answer grounded academic questions with plain language and recommend official sources when the answer depends on current approval or availability.
Can a student just add a class because they want a certain teacher?

No, not as a normal basis for a change. The handbook and course catalog both say requests based on wanting a particular teacher are not likely to be granted or will not be granted. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
I want to drop something, but I am worried it will look like I could not handle it.

Sometimes dropping a class looks weaker. Sometimes keeping the wrong class and dragging down the rest of the year looks worse. The answer depends on whether the class is still a good fit and what it is costing the rest of your schedule.
So the real question is fit, not pride?
Yes. Pride is a bad substitute for judgment.
I am deciding between Robotics and Debate, and both sound impressive in different ways.

Then stop thinking about how they sound and think about the actual afternoons. Do you want design, building, testing, coding, and engineering teamwork, or do you want speaking, argument, refutation, and strategic reasoning?
That makes the choice feel more real.
Good. Real choices get clearer when you picture the work, not the label.
I need to ask if I am in the wrong level, but I do not want to sound panicked or dramatic.
Keep the message focused on fit and judgment.
Can you give me a version?

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask for your honest view on whether my current level in [course] still seems like the right fit. I have been trying to assess it carefully, and I would appreciate your perspective. Best, [Your Name]
What does Webb say about who students should go to first for academic help?
Webb says the classroom teacher is the student's primary source of assistance and guidance. [source: course catalog] [source: handbook]
Can parents ask to see school records?
Yes. The handbook says parents have the right to inspect their child's school records. [source: handbook]
Does Webb say tutors are paid by the school or by families?
Families pay them directly. Webb says tutors are independent contractors and are paid directly by families. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
What does Webb say about student publications?

Webb says student publications are educational in purpose and are meant to help students develop craft, voice, and responsible expression. Faculty advisors guide the work, and the school reserves the right not to publish material that conflicts with its mission, values, or student code of conduct. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about honor in one clear sentence?
Webb says honor is the cornerstone of the community and is based on honesty, integrity, respect, and fairness for self and others. [source: handbook]
What does Webb expect students to do if something unsafe or wrong is happening in front of them?
Webb expects students to take reasonable action: speak up, make sure appropriate people are informed, and leave the scene with others if needed. [source: handbook]
Does Webb say anything specific about gender-neutral bathrooms?
Yes. The handbook says gender-neutral bathrooms are available in multiple campus locations and are available to all students. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about names and pronouns for transgender students?

The handbook says students have the right to be addressed by the name and pronoun that correspond to their gender identity, and the school does not tolerate intentional and persistent refusal to respect that identity. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about discriminatory conduct?
The handbook says discriminatory or harassing conduct, whether on or off campus or through social media, is a major school offense. [source: handbook]
What does Webb say about using a phone in the dining hall?
The handbook says cell phones should not be present in the dining hall during any time. [source: handbook]
